page.title: Marbella Sunset Tapas Cruise: Evening Flavors on the Sea

data.meta_description: Book a Marbella sunset tapas cruise from Puerto Banús. Enjoy Spanish flavours, drinks & sea views from €749/2h. Reserve your evening charter now.
data.summary: A Marbella sunset tapas cruise combines Spanish bites, drinks, and coastal views aboard a private motor yacht from Puerto Banús. Rates from €749 for 2 hours include skipper, fuel, and a full tapas menu.
key_facts: Prices from €749 for 2 hours (Tier A boats) up to €4,719 for 4 hours (Mangusta 80)
Includes licensed skipper, fuel, drinks (water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava), and light snacks
Tapas menu served on board (varies by charter length)
Maximum 9–12 guests depending on boat selected
Private charter only – no shared groups

faq.q: What is included in a Marbella sunset tapas cruise?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). The tapas menu is served on board and varies by charter length – typically 3–5 Spanish tapas items plus bread and olives.

faq.q: How much does a sunset tapas cruise cost in Marbella?
faq.a: Prices depend on the boat. Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) cost €749 for 2 hours or €2,299 for 8 hours. Tier B boats (Mangusta 80, white/grey) cost €4,719 minimum 4 hours and include a jet ski. Other boats are priced on request via WhatsApp.

faq.q: Which boats are available for a sunset tapas cruise?
faq.a: We offer a range of motor yachts from Puerto Banús, including the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax), Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax), Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax), Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax), and many more. All depart from Puerto Banús.

faq.q: What time does a sunset tapas cruise depart?
faq.a: Sunset cruises are typically scheduled around 19:00–21:00, depending on the season and sunset time. We can adjust departure times to match the golden hour. Contact us via WhatsApp to confirm availability.

faq.q: Can I customise the tapas menu on board?
faq.a: Yes, the tapas menu can be tailored to your preferences – vegetarian, gluten-free, or specific Spanish dishes. Just let us know in advance. The standard menu includes 3–5 tapas items plus bread, olives, and drinks.

faq.q: Is the sunset tapas cruise private?
faq.a: Yes, every charter is private – you and your guests only. No shared groups. You can choose the boat, duration, and menu. The skipper handles navigation while you relax and enjoy the sunset.

faq.q: What happens if the weather is bad?
faq.a: If adverse weather is forecast, we will contact you to reschedule or offer a full refund. Safety is our priority. The skipper makes the final call on the day.

faq.q: Do I need to bring anything for the sunset cruise?
faq.a: Just yourself, a light jacket (evenings can be cool), and sunscreen if you're on deck. All food, drinks, and safety equipment are provided. We recommend arriving 15 minutes before departure at Puerto Banús.

data.body_html:
As the sun drops behind La Concha and the Mediterranean turns gold, few experiences rival a Marbella sunset tapas cruise. Departing from Puerto Banús, you'll sip chilled cava, share Spanish tapas, and watch the coastline fade into dusk – all from a private motor yacht. Whether you're celebrating an anniversary or simply escaping the evening crowds, this is Marbella at its most memorable. Prices start from €749 for a 2-hour charter, including skipper, fuel, drinks, and tapas.

Why Choose a Sunset Tapas Cruise in Marbella?

Marbella's evening light is its own attraction. From the deck of a private yacht, you'll see the Golden Mile, the marina lights of Puerto Banús, and the Sierra Blanca mountains turning pink. A sunset tapas cruise combines three things the Costa del Sol does best: good food, good company, and the sea. You avoid crowded beach clubs and terrace queues – instead, you anchor in a quiet cove like Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés, where the only sounds are water lapping and glasses clinking.

What's Included in Your Sunset Tapas Charter

Every charter with us includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). The tapas menu is served on board – typically 3–5 Spanish dishes such as patatas bravas, croquetas, jamón ibérico, gambas al ajillo, and pimientos de padrón, plus bread and olives. For longer charters, we can arrange a more extensive menu. All dietary requirements can be accommodated if advised in advance.

Our Boats for Sunset Tapas Cruises

We operate a fleet of motor yachts from Puerto Banús, all suitable for sunset cruising. For a couple or small group, the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) or Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) are ideal – both priced at €749 for 2 hours. For a larger celebration, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax, white or grey) offers space and a free jet ski from €4,719 for 4 hours. Other boats include the Azimut 58 Flybridge, Fairline Targa 12m, Pershing 46, and the Ferretti 94 – all depart from Puerto Banús. For pricing on these, please contact us via WhatsApp.

Typical Sunset Cruise Itinerary

A 2-hour sunset cruise usually heads west towards Cabopino or east towards the Marbella coastline, depending on wind and sunset position. You'll motor slowly, anchor for 30–40 minutes, and enjoy the tapas as the sun sets. A 4-hour charter allows time to reach Estepona or Sotogrande, with a longer anchor stop. The skipper sets the pace – you decide how much time you want at anchor versus cruising. Departure is typically 19:00 in summer, earlier in spring and autumn.

Prices at a Glance

BoatDurationPrice (all inclusive)
Astondoa 40 / Azimut 392 hours€749
Astondoa 40 / Azimut 398 hours€2,299
Mangusta 80 (white/grey)4 hours€4,719 (includes jet ski)
All other boatsOn requestContact via WhatsApp

All prices include skipper, fuel, drinks, tapas, insurance, and 21% IVA. No hidden extras.

How to Book Your Sunset Tapas Cruise

Booking is straightforward. Choose your preferred boat and duration, then contact us via WhatsApp to confirm availability. We recommend booking at least 48 hours in advance, especially in July and August. A 50% deposit secures the charter, with the balance due on the day. Cancellation is free up to 72 hours before departure. For more ideas on what to do on the water, explore our Marbella boat rental hub.

Frequently asked questions

What is included in a Marbella sunset tapas cruise?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). The tapas menu is served on board and varies by charter length – typically 3–5 Spanish tapas items plus bread and olives.

How much does a sunset tapas cruise cost in Marbella?

Prices depend on the boat. Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) cost €749 for 2 hours or €2,299 for 8 hours. Tier B boats (Mangusta 80, white/grey) cost €4,719 minimum 4 hours and include a jet ski. Other boats are priced on request via WhatsApp.

Which boats are available for a sunset tapas cruise?

We offer a range of motor yachts from Puerto Banús, including the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax), Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax), Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax), Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax), and many more. All depart from Puerto Banús.

What time does a sunset tapas cruise depart?

Sunset cruises are typically scheduled around 19:00–21:00, depending on the season and sunset time. We can adjust departure times to match the golden hour. Contact us via WhatsApp to confirm availability.

Can I customise the tapas menu on board?

Yes, the tapas menu can be tailored to your preferences – vegetarian, gluten-free, or specific Spanish dishes. Just let us know in advance. The standard menu includes 3–5 tapas items plus bread, olives, and drinks.

Is the sunset tapas cruise private?

Yes, every charter is private – you and your guests only. No shared groups. You can choose the boat, duration, and menu. The skipper handles navigation while you relax and enjoy the sunset.

What happens if the weather is bad?

If adverse weather is forecast, we will contact you to reschedule or offer a full refund. Safety is our priority. The skipper makes the final call on the day.

Do I need to bring anything for the sunset cruise?

Just yourself, a light jacket (evenings can be cool), and sunscreen if you're on deck. All food, drinks, and safety equipment are provided. We recommend arriving 15 minutes before departure at Puerto Banús.
